title: Three.js viewport — scene + mesh rendering
analysis:
This task creates the Three.js 3D viewport for the Reify GUI. The gui/ directory already has a well-structured SolidJS app with Tauri bridge, engineStore (which holds meshes as Record<string, MeshData>), theme tokens (viewportBg: '#181825'), and comprehensive test patterns using vitest + @solidjs/testing-library + jsdom.

Key observations:
1. MeshData uses typed arrays (Float32Array for vertices/normals, Uint32Array for indices) — maps directly to Three.js BufferGeometry attributes.
2. engineStore.state.meshes is a Record<string, MeshData> keyed by entity_path — meshManager needs to diff this to create/update/remove Three.js Mesh objects.
3. jsdom doesn't support WebGL, so all Three.js classes must be mocked in tests. We test logic/wiring, not actual rendering.
4. The app uses pnpm (pnpm-lock.yaml exists), so we use pnpm for dependency installation.
5. Theme provides viewportBg (#181825) for the renderer background.
6. The task description mentions 'distinct colors per entity_path' — we'll use a deterministic color palette (hash entity_path to pick from a set of Catppuccin-style accent colors).

Architecture:
- scene.ts: Factory function returning {scene, camera, renderer, lights}. Sets up PerspectiveCamera, ambient+directional lights, GridHelper, AxesHelper, and configures renderer with dark background.
- controls.ts: Thin wrapper around OrbitControls, exposing orbit/pan/zoom with sensible defaults and a dispose method.
- meshManager.ts: Pure logic class that maintains a Map<string, THREE.Mesh> and provides a sync(meshes: Record<string, MeshData>) method that diffs the current Three.js meshes against incoming data.
- Viewport.tsx: SolidJS component with a canvas ref, initializes scene/renderer/controls onMount, uses createEffect to subscribe to engineStore.meshes, handles resize, runs requestAnimationFrame loop, cleans up onCleanup.

prerequisites.description: Add three.js dependency to gui/package.json: 'three' in dependencies, '@types/three' in devDependencies. Run pnpm install to update lockfile.
Create gui/src/viewport/ and gui/src/__tests__/viewport/ directory structure.

steps.description: Write failing tests for scene.ts in gui/src/__tests__/viewport/scene.test.ts: (1) createScene returns object with scene, camera, renderer properties, (2) scene contains AmbientLight and DirectionalLight children, (3) camera is PerspectiveCamera with reasonable FOV/near/far defaults, (4) renderer setClearColor was called with theme viewportBg color, (5) scene contains GridHelper, (6) scene contains AxesHelper, (7) resize() updates camera aspect and renderer size. Mock THREE.WebGLRenderer (construct mock, track setClearColor/setSize/setPixelRatio calls) since jsdom lacks WebGL context. Create minimal mock factory for THREE classes.

Implement scene.ts: createScene(canvas: HTMLCanvasElement, width: number, height: number) factory that creates PerspectiveCamera (fov 60, near 0.1, far 10000), AmbientLight (0.4 intensity), DirectionalLight (0.8 intensity, positioned at [5,10,7]), WebGLRenderer (antialias, canvas, devicePixelRatio, clear color from THEME_TOKENS.viewportBg), GridHelper (size 20, divisions 20, subtle grid colors), AxesHelper (size 2). Returns { scene, camera, renderer, resize(w,h) }.

Write failing tests for controls.ts in gui/src/__tests__/viewport/controls.test.ts: (1) createControls returns object with update and dispose methods, (2) constructor is called with camera and domElement, (3) enableDamping is set to true, (4) dispose calls controls.dispose(). Mock OrbitControls from three/addons/controls/OrbitControls.js.

Implement controls.ts: createControls(camera, domElement) wrapper that instantiates OrbitControls with enableDamping=true, dampingFactor=0.1, reasonable min/maxDistance. Returns { controls, update(), dispose() }.

Write failing tests for meshManager.ts in gui/src/__tests__/viewport/meshManager.test.ts: (1) createMeshManager returns object with sync/dispose/getSceneMeshes methods, (2) sync({entityPath: meshData}) creates a THREE.Mesh and adds it to scene (scene.add called), (3) the created Mesh's geometry has position attribute set from MeshData.vertices (Float32Array), index set from MeshData.indices, and normal attribute set from MeshData.normals, (4) sync with updated vertices calls setAttribute on existing geometry rather than creating new Mesh, (5) sync with a removed entity_path calls geometry.dispose(), material.dispose(), scene.remove() for that mesh, (6) each entity_path gets a deterministic color (same path = same color, different paths can get different colors), (7) dispose() removes and disposes all meshes from scene, (8) sync with MeshData where normals is null creates geometry without normal attribute. Mock THREE.BufferGeometry, THREE.Mesh, THREE.MeshStandardMaterial, THREE.BufferAttribute, THREE.Scene.

Implement meshManager.ts: createMeshManager(scene: THREE.Scene) maintains Map<string, THREE.Mesh>. sync(meshes: Record<string, MeshData>) diffs current map vs incoming: creates new Mesh objects (BufferGeometry with Float32BufferAttribute for position/normal, index from Uint32Array, MeshStandardMaterial with deterministic color), updates existing ones (geometry.setAttribute), removes stale ones (geometry.dispose(), material.dispose(), scene.remove()). Color palette uses Catppuccin accent colors [blue #89b4fa, mauve #cba6f7, green #a6e3a1, peach #fab387, red #f38ba8, teal #94e2d5, yellow #f9e2af, pink #f5c2e7] cycled by simple string hash of entity_path. getSceneMeshes() returns current Map for inspection. dispose() cleans up everything.

Write failing tests for Viewport.tsx in gui/src/__tests__/viewport/Viewport.test.tsx: (1) Viewport renders a <canvas> element with data-testid='viewport-canvas', (2) canvas is wrapped in a container div with data-testid='viewport-container' that has width/height 100% styling. Mock all three.js imports (scene, controls, meshManager modules) since we're testing the SolidJS component lifecycle/DOM, not WebGL. Use @solidjs/testing-library render + screen.getByTestId.

Implement Viewport.tsx: SolidJS component that creates a container div (100% width/height) with a <canvas> ref inside. onMount: initialize createScene(canvas, w, h), createControls(camera, renderer.domElement), createMeshManager(scene). Use createEffect watching a meshes prop (Record<string, MeshData>) to call meshManager.sync(). Set up ResizeObserver on the container div to call scene resize + renderer.setSize. Start requestAnimationFrame render loop (controls.update + renderer.render(scene, camera)). onCleanup: cancel RAF, dispose controls, dispose meshManager, dispose renderer, disconnect ResizeObserver. Implement index.ts to export Viewport component, and re-export createScene, createControls, createMeshManager for testability.

design_decisions.decision: Mock all Three.js classes in tests rather than using a WebGL stub/headless renderer
design_decisions.rationale: jsdom doesn't support WebGL at all. Using gl/headless-gl adds heavy native dependencies. Mocking lets us test logic and wiring while keeping the test environment lightweight and consistent with the existing vitest+jsdom setup.

design_decisions.decision: Use a deterministic hash of entity_path for mesh color assignment rather than random colors
design_decisions.rationale: Ensures the same entity always gets the same color across re-renders and sessions, improving visual consistency. Uses a small palette of Catppuccin accent colors cycled by a simple string hash.

design_decisions.decision: meshManager uses a sync(record) pattern instead of individual add/remove methods
design_decisions.rationale: The engineStore provides meshes as a Record<string, MeshData>. A single sync call that diffs the full record simplifies the reactive wiring — just call sync(state.meshes) in a createEffect. This mirrors the SolidJS store pattern where the whole record is the reactive granule.

design_decisions.decision: Scene setup separated into its own module (scene.ts) rather than inline in Viewport.tsx
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the SolidJS component thin (lifecycle management only) and makes the Three.js scene configuration independently testable and reusable.

design_decisions.decision: Use ResizeObserver on the canvas container rather than window resize event
design_decisions.rationale: The viewport panel can resize independently of the window (e.g., when panels are resized). ResizeObserver accurately tracks the actual container dimensions.

design_decisions.decision: AxesHelper in main scene rather than a separate overlay scene/camera for axis gizmo
design_decisions.rationale: A corner-pinned axis gizmo via secondary scene adds significant complexity. For v0.1, an AxesHelper in the main scene provides orientation reference with minimal code. Can be upgraded to a corner overlay later.

design_decisions.decision: three added to dependencies (not devDependencies), @types/three to devDependencies
design_decisions.rationale: Three.js is needed at runtime for the 3D viewport. @types/three is only needed for TypeScript compilation.

design_decisions.decision: Viewport accepts meshes as a prop rather than directly importing engineStore
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the Viewport component decoupled from the specific store implementation, making it easier to test (pass mock data) and reuse. The parent component (App.tsx or a layout component) is responsible for wiring engineStore.state.meshes to the Viewport prop.

reuse.what: MeshData / RawMeshData types with typed arrays (Float32Array, Uint32Array)
reuse.how: Import MeshData interface directly — its Float32Array/Uint32Array fields map perfectly to Three.js BufferAttribute. No conversion needed.

reuse.what: engineStore with Record<string, MeshData> meshes state
reuse.how: Viewport.tsx will accept meshes: Record<string, MeshData> as a prop. The parent wires engineStore.state.meshes to this prop. meshManager.sync() consumes the full record.
Import THEME_TOKENS to set Three.js renderer clear color, ensuring visual consistency with the CSS theme.
Follow the same mocking pattern for dependencies. Use vi.mock for Three.js modules. Use render() + screen.getByTestId() from @solidjs/testing-library for component tests.
No changes needed — existing config supports TSX transforms and jsdom environment which is sufficient for mocked Three.js tests.

reuse.what: Catppuccin accent colors from theme tokens
reuse.how: Use THEME_TOKENS accent colors as the base palette for mesh material colors, extending with additional Catppuccin accent colors for variety.
